# Transcrição: 08-ACTIVITY- Creating Surprise in Your Professional or Business Story

- **Fonte:** 08-ACTIVITY- Creating Surprise in Your Professional or Business Story
- **Duração:** 02:07
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:07]

Now I want you to do the same for your professional story. In admission, there's fewer surprises in professional stories. It's just the way it is. It doesn't mean that there aren't surprises. It doesn't mean there should not be surprises. Think about Steve Jobs. One more thing was always his surprise. It was the final thing that was both astounding and he saved it to last. He understood how to surprise his audience. You will have surprises in every presentation you give, no matter what you do, if you're smart. You'll have surprises in every marketing plan, every advertising campaign, every pitch to investors. All of these should contain moments of surprise. If you don't have them, you're not delighting your audience. Surprise can be a feature that is unexpected on a product that you've designed. It can be a method of delivering a service to a customer that is both unexpected and delightful. Surprise can be something as simple as, I've created a marketing deck and I've made sure that at the end of one slide, it asks an interesting question that will be answered on the next slide in an interesting way. That is a wonderful way to create a little surprise as you're working through 32 slides of a marketing deck and hoping to keep the audience engaged. By making sure that you're constantly providing the unexpected during a presentation, you'll be providing surprise. So take a look at that professional story that you've been working on. Identify moments for possible surprise. Identify moments where your customers, your clients, your people, your investors might not be expecting the thing you're about to say. And then find a way to maximize that surprise. Find a way to say it in a way that is especially surprising or find a way to build the information earlier on so that surprise can pay off. Do the same thing that you did with your personal story for your professional story now.
